title: dagaladoo XVX 131 Keys Double Shot Keycaps, Cherry Profile PBT Keycaps Full Set, Custom Keyboard Keycaps for 60% 65% 75% 100% Cherry Gateron MX Switches Mechanical Keyboard, Dark Blue/Black/White
features: Full 131 keys custom keycaps set, including 108 standard keys and 23 modifiers keys, suitable for most ASIN US and UK layout 61/64/66/68/84/87/96/98/100/104/108 mechanical keyboards.
Cherry profile keycaps, ergonomic shapes design and curved layout, provide a lasting and comfortable experience of typing to you.
Double shot design keycaps, made from two colors material molded into each others, make the keycaps characters maintain the vibrance and saturation, clear and not fade.
Made from high content PBT material, 1.3mm extra thick keycaps wall, which is provide top-notch durability, excellent touch and grease resistance.
Minimalist theme style and classic color matching keycaps, will be a powerful addition to for personalized of your keyboards.

reviews.title: Great budget keycaps
reviews.text: Please don’t mind my cat lol she’s modeling the keyboard. Really great budget keycaps, super pretty color. I use aqua king switches and the sound with the keycaps is pretty good. Really like and enjoy them. P.S. the yellow keycaps ARE NOT part of the set, they are from another keycap set. I thought it would compliment the purple nicely :)

reviews.title: Nice keycaps, but holy crap they're snug
reviews.text:
These keycaps are probably best suited for soldered switches. If you're on a hot swap board, get ready to separate every key from the cap as you'll be pulling the entire assembly out EVERY TIME.

Other than my tolerance/keycap ripping issues these are solid and have a good sound indicative of a good pbt material.

reviews.title: Great keycap set, just wish they we're backlight compatible.
reviews.text: That's my fault, I always assume keycaps are backlight compatible but they feel awesome, love the satin touch, they look great, and the legend is super bright and clear. If you're not specifically looking for a keycap set to use with a backlit keyboard, this one looks great.

title: Gaming Keyboard and Mouse Combo, MageGee GK770 RGB Backlit Wired Gaming Keyboard with Multimedia Keys, Ergonomic Wrist Rest Computer Keyboard, 3200 DPI Gaming Mouse for Windows PC Gamers
features: ✅GAMING KEYBOARD & MOUSE: A standard 104 keys layout plus 10 multimedia function keys (total 114 keys), gives faster response and better tactile feedback. Accurate, comfortable, soft touch with anti-ghosting functionality. RGB backlit effect provides a unique visual experience, taking you into a pretty cool gaming atmosphere. Ergonomic wrist rest offers enough support and protection to your hands, and reduce hand fatigue caused by long-term use.

✅10 MULTIMEDIA KEYS & 25 NON-CONFLICT KEYS: 10 independent multimedia keys at the top of the keyboard makes it quick access to music player, email, computer, calculator without interrupting your game. 25 anti-ghosting keys allow you to press or hold multiple keys simultaneously, helping you win in the fierce game without being bound by speed. Adjustable rear feet and non-slip pads provide comfortable typing experience.

✅RGB LED BACKLIGHT KEYBOARD: The keyboard has RGB light settings with multiple modes (dynamic backlit or solid static light) to choose from, making it shining and sparkling in the dark, easy to find the correct keys and reduce error rate. You can adjust backlight brightness and speed according to personal preference. The backlight also can be turned off / on by pressing Fn + Page Down.

✅ADJUSTABLE DPI GAMING MOUSE: 7 colors LED breathing backlit ( blue, yellow, green, white, purple, red, cyan), 4 adjustable DPI - 600/800/1200/1600/2400/3200 to match the mouse speed instantly for different game scenarios. Advanced gaming optical sensor ensures accurate positioning and stable transmission. Pressing the DPI button for 3 seconds can turn off the mouse backlight. Contoured shape fits your hands perfectly, comfortable to use.

✅WIDE COMPATIBILITY: The USB wired keyboard and mouse combo is simple to use, just plug and play, no need for additional driver or software. Works well with Win2000, Win XP, WinME, Vista, Win7, Win8, Android, linux, Mac and other operating systems. Suitable for gaming beginners or lovers.

reviews.title: nice but mouse dont work
reviews.text: it was pretty and bright but the mouse didn't work with my pc.

reviews.title: Complete JUNK----Don't waste your money
reviews.text: Button's Stick very bad mouse makes your characters run where ever they want too it only last a few months then its garbage

reviews.title: Beautiful
reviews.text: This is the most adorable keyboard. I love it for work and for gaming. It has a lovely feel to it and the colors are great! The only thing that i would change is the sound of the keys when you are typing as it is sort of loud. Sometimes when I am typing really fast I can not hear the other person on the phone speak.

reviews.title: Birthday gift
reviews.text: I got this to go with my sons new desktop that he got for his 10th birthday.  He absolutely loves it and has yet to have any issues.  Well worth the purchase.

reviews.title: stopped working after a few months
reviews.text: love the keyboard, only reason for the bad review is that the mouse stopped working less than 6 months after i bought it, tried to get a hold of them but they dont have any sort of contact so now i have to get a new mouse

title: DAIDAI AJAZZ Diced Fruit Blueberry Keyboard Switch | 5 Pin 35gf Light Force Pre Lubed Linear Switch | Durable 80M MX Switch for Mechanical Keyboard (36PCS | Blueberry Switch)
features: ♫♫♫AJAZZ Diced Fruit blueberry switch for mechanical keyboard top cap and button part of the housing are made of transparent light blue and dark blue, blue-gray POM shaft core adds dust-proof wall, which increases the stability when pressing, and provides you with a smoother typing experience.

♫♫♫5 pin keyboard blueberry switches is the latest light pressure linear switch are made with operating force of 35gf and total travel of 3.8mm, 15.5mm double spring is more smooth when typing, it is perfectly compatible with 5pin hotswap mechanical keyboard.

♫♫♫Mechanical keyboard switch adopts MX structure design, which is widely compatible with most keycap sets with (X) crossbar, LED card slot and transparent PC material, which enhances the light transmission of keyboard LED lights, provide more choices and fun for fans who like personalized keyboard. (Note: Switch opener not included)

♫♫♫Prelubed linear switches in order to improve the smoothness and durability, it has been lubricated before leaving the factor, and the rebound is faster and smoother when the switch is pressed. The experimental test has exceeded 80 million keystrokes.

♫♫♫Friendly packaging design, open the packaging with a single switch, in order to experience the click feel to DIY enthusiasts, if you don't like it, you can return it, the remaining 35PCS switches are stored in transparent round cans, a bit like canned fruit, it is more convenient to store and carry it, perfect for gifting to yourself, friends or family.

♫♫♫AJAZZ fruit series custom switches in order to satisfy you who like DIY, we introduce different tactile or linear switches, whether beginners, professional gamers, typists and programmers can choose the most suitable AJAZZ fruit series switches. Create a unique typing experience for you.

reviews.title: Light, snappy, like a punchier Gat brown
reviews.text:
I previously got a tactile switch sampler set and honestly loved the AJAZZ Banana more than U4 Boba and its other iterations. I liked these more than Glorious Panda and Holy Panda. I type a lot as a student, so I didn't want anything too heavy--it gets tiring. I've been using AJAZZ Banana for two days so far now and I LOVE it!

I was using lubed Gateron browns before. I wanted something punchier but not too heavy. Banana is the perfect answer to that. Actuation force is about the same to Gat browns. I have a north-facing RGB board and the lights come through Banana switches beautifully.

In answer to the other reviewer, I believe that the Banana switches ARE lubed (with what, I don't know). I have a stock/un-lubed AJAZZ Banana switch from my switch sampler pack, these pre-lubed ones feel slightly lighter in comparison. Out of the box, these feel great without additional lubing. Results will vary depending on your mech keeb setup.

Note that the switch pack comes with 46 switches, so it won't be enough to do a full board. I have a 60% board and had enough to do the alphanumeric keys and other frequently pressed keys like Shift, Enter, Ctrl, Backspace, Alt, Tab, and punctuation keys. I left my Gat browns on the less frequent keys. I have two "extra" Banana switches as backups.

Highly recommend. If you want tactile but not heavy, this is a very affordable option.

reviews.title: Bananas are excellent tactile switch alternatives to browns and blues.
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:dff950435419a7006b0295e50b846c44]] This review is for the Ajazz Diced Fruit Banana Switch.
I wanted to try these switches compared to a brown switch because of its similar characteristics.
These Banana switches are definitely tactile and smooth. They do have a tactile click sound and require a bit more force to actuate (not really noticeable for me because I'm heavy handed and I bottom out the key when I type). I'm not going to mention specs such as actuation force and distance because you can find that info in the product description or manufacturer's site.
I will talk about the "feeling" of these switches though because in everyday use, gaming and office work, I'm not thinking about the actuation force/distance, etc. For me it is how it feels and sounds.

So first off, these are excellent switches, no doubt. It is then just a matter of personal preference whether or not you like the characteristics of the Banana switch. For me, it is a bit too noisy. The tactile bump feels good but after using the Banana's for a while, I would hate typing while on mic because the mic picks up the keystrokes loud and clear and can be annoying for others to hear. I don't have blue switches to compare these Bananas to but I would imagine the blue switches would be worse in sound. I do have red switches to compare and the reds are noticeably quieter, I think mainly due to being linear (no tactile bump). For me and personal preference and usage, I would stick with the reds.

If you like tactile switches and are currently using a different tactile switch, I'd urge you give these Banana switches a try. See my video review where I installed these switches into a keyboard and compare to red switches, so you can get an idea of the sound when the switches are installed into a keyboard frame with keycaps on. In the video I have mistakenly called the red switches Gaterons, but I'm pretty sure they are Kailh reds with box stem.

reviews.title: Nice looking strong tactile
reviews.text:
I love tactile switches, so when this was offered for review I figured I'd give it a shot as I'd heard good things about Ajazz switches. It arrived and I swapped out the switches in my NK65 (DROP Holy Pandas) and threw these into the alphas.

In terms of feel they're a pretty strong tactile. I felt it important to mention that I was using HP's above because these feel very similar to those, just with a lighter spring. The bump is close to identical which is nice, and I prefer the lighter spring as I've been mainlining my Niz lately with 35g switches, so light is my thing right now. In terms of sound, the housing does make these quite a bit higher pitch than HP's, and I'd honestly put it in line with some Akko tactiles. Not too high to not enjoy, but higher than I'm initially used to.

Because these come pre-lubed they can be used stock, which is nice because while once I start I find lubing switches to be relaxing, but it's an activity I dread until I'm actually going. Just something about it seems beyond tedious, and to be fair, it is. Thankfully the lube job on these is pretty good and there's no ping to be heard or scratchiness to be felt. I did lube one just to see the difference that one done by hand can make, and the job on these is good enough that it makes no difference. The switch is fine as-is.

Other than being plate mount only I can't find a problem with these. The spring weight is nice and they sound and feel great to type on. I'm thinking about grabbing another set to build out a board with them in the future.

reviews.title: Kiwi switches
reviews.text:
This is a review for the Kiwi Switch from DAIDAI AJAZZ. The Kiwi switch is tactile, 50gf, and housed in glittery translucent green. If you have clear keycaps, you can see the yellow stem and a little of the upper housing.

For the Kiwi switches, you get 46 switches. For most keyboards, 46 switches is not enough and you will need 2-3 cans.

The switches come in a clear plastic can, with a screw lid and a pull tab. The packaging is cute! More importantly, the can takes up less space than the usual plastic tray, and it’s reusable.

I use Akko Vintage Whites (linear), but I wanted to try tactile switches for the thumb clusters on my ergo keyboard, so I had feedback when I pressed those keys. The tactile bump is smooth and pronounced, I definitely know I pressed that key.

I didn’t notice any difference between these pre-lubed Kiwi switches and non-lubed switches.

reviews.title: Pretty good tactile switches
reviews.text: I've tried linears and even clicky switches, but I really prefer tacticles. These are right in the sweet spot for me in terms of spring weight and tactile bump. Nice colors, if that matters to you. I got these to try out before buying a full set, and I like them quite a bit.

reviews.title: Just what I was looking for
reviews.text: Great quality, love that it comes with multiple different colors of the same key so that you can customize it. So happy with this purchase!!!

reviews.title: Good feeling, good overall design
reviews.text: Was able to use these keys on my gaming keyboard.  It provided a good feeling as I press on the keys.  It’s Very responsive.  The only downside to this is that the rgb lights on my keyboard does not pass through the keys…not the end of the world but overall a good set of keys.  The picture on the website is deceiving as it’s just keys to replace an existing keyboard.  It does not come with the keyboard itself.

reviews.title: You Like Red, White & Blue So Make Your Keyboard Reflect That!
reviews.text: We started doing this with our “high-end” computer buyers. We take a premium – Cherry or similar – keyboard and remove all or most of the key tops replacing them with the various colors available making a color complementary computer input device. It makes for a more interesting keyboard, which obviously is the main interface with your computer, and it gives our customers something so unique as to be one-of-a-kind. All the keyboards start with premium red, blue, brown, or black key actuators which, without exception, are the best available. That together with our customer’s keytops “artwork” gives a person something exclusive as well as offering superior functionality. The keytops come right off use the small plastic tool most of the keytop provides include with their kits and doing a whole keyboard only takes about 20 minutes. One thing you do have to watch for is the keytop height. Many times, they will make certain keys a little higher. This poses no issue unless you stick a high key in the middle of the number/letters, then this may impede faster typists. Remember each key actuator that is attached to the base of the keyboard is programmed to send a certain signal to the computer thus causing your monitor to display that letter/number or take that action. For example, if you put the “M” keytop where the “T” was that will still send a “T” to your computer and monitor. I would recommend removing and replacing 2 or 3 at a time or having a like keyboard to refer to, so the possibility for confusion is limited. All keyboards don’t have the exact layout especially in the extended section and number pad. Also, some of the higher end gaming style keyboards have extra programmable keys that are specified through the keyboard manufacturer’s software. Some of the keytop set will provide blank keys to use in these instances. Buy a couple sets and have fun making your own custom keyboard.

reviews.title: Great color for white keyboard, good quality
reviews.text: The NACODEX 129 Keys PBT Keycaps Set is a high-quality and visually appealing upgrade for any mechanical keyboard. The keycaps are made of durable PBT material and feature a JSA profile and double shot design. The pink and white color combination gives a unique touch to the keyboard, making it a great choice for those who want to personalize their setup. The keycaps are compatible with Cherry, Gateron, Otemu, and Kailh switches, making them a versatile choice for many different types of mechanical keyboards. Overall, this keycap set is a great investment for anyone looking to improve the look and feel of their keyboard.

reviews.title: !!!!!Great PCB!!!!!!!!

Fit perfectly, No problems soldering, and definitely came out perfect out of the box, though I don't know how to brighten up the LED nor change the modes, I'm completely fine with the RGB cycle. I'm Typing on my new keyboard as we speak and it works perfectly, no delays, no nothing, just perfect!!!!

(I can admit I'm kinda too last to do VIA so I'm just working with it lol)

reviews.title: Not doublshot
reviews.text:
These two keys: < and > have already worn through. These are not double shot keycaps. They are a semi-translucent keycap with a finish over it that wears through. Doubleshot has an injection molded insert inside the keycap with the translucent area cut out of the keycap.
Should you not care about that, here is some more info. They look okay in the light, but in the dark there is a strong screen glare off of them that can be distracting.

I rate one star for lying about the nature of the product.

reviews.title: eh, cheap.  removed them almost instantly.
reviews.text: slippery, their feel is very cheap and i ended up removing them and regretting my buy.  i have bought other replacment keys before.

reviews.title: I didn't try them...
reviews.text: The color wasn't what I expected so I didn't open the very nice plastic packaging before returning.

reviews.title: DOES NOT FIT CORSAIR STRAFE RGB.
reviews.text: Overall, it's pretty cool. I like it a lot. It is very smudge, and the foil is cheap so it wont last all that long.

reviews.title: Arrived missing key caps but overall pretty good if you have the right keyboard
reviews.text:
Not a keyboard, just key caps to "bling out" your existing keyboard.

I purchased the following keyboard and these key caps fit perfectly. The keyboard (not this item, which is only key caps) comes with a red plastic tool that you can use to pop out the key caps:

Redragon K551 Mechanical Gaming Keyboard RGB LED Rainbow Backlit Wired Keyboard with Red Switches for Windows Gaming PC (104 Keys, Black)

Even the font on the key caps matches the keyboard I bought separately, and the backlighting shines through the font.

The only down side: the set of key caps is missing about 1/4 of the keys (see attached picture).

I would give it all five stars if it had arrived containing all the key caps advertised in the product listing. It was a surprise prank gift for a coworker, which is why I made do with what we got.

title: Ganss ALT 71 Wireless/Wired 2.4G + USB Mechanical Keyboard with Cherry MX Clear Switch for Mac and Windows, PBT Keycaps for Desktop and Laptop Computer, 71 Keys (Can Be Customize to 68 Keys Layout)
features: Cherry MX Clear Switches - Medium Tactile and Light Clicky. They are ideal for people who want to use their keyboard for both gaming and typing.

Compact and Portable - Enjoy the freedom of wirelessness with a 2.4G connection. Reliable and fast connects with devices. It saves desk space and fits right in the backpack to carry around. Perfect for home, work and on the go.
Full Keys Anti-ghosting - All 71 keys are conflict free N-Key rollover with no input error for ultimate Gaming performance, it’s more friendly to users, ensuring smooth gaming experience.
Wireless keyboard - Simply connect your portable keyboard with one USB receiver without any software.

PBT Dye Sublimation - With high-quality PBT keycaps and an anti-grease coating, these 71-key keycaps provide top-notch durability and a great feel for your typing. Advanced five-sided sublimation technology makes the pattern not fade.

reviews.title: Only the 3 switches above the arrow keys are hotswapable
reviews.text:
Ganss ALT 71 Wireless/Wired 2.4G + USB Mechanical Keyboard with Cherry MX Brown Switch for Mac and Windows, PBT Keycaps for Desktop and Laptop Computer, 71 Keys (Can Be Customize to 68 Keys Layout)

This Keyboard is weird. I don't know why they made only the 3 switches above the arrow key hotswapable and not the others. I like the customization of the layout, but c'mon. The keyboard sounds terrible and with the limited tuning I was able to do, it sounds and feels a bit better. The wireless works fine and doesn't lose connection. The included keycaps are not great either. I replaced those straight away. If you are looking for a keyboard to mod this is not it.

reviews.title: Compact and functional at a premium price
reviews.text:
The Ganss ALT 71 keyboard is good, but not good enough to justify the price tag. I do really like that it's wireless--the dongle works well and I didn't experience any lag while typing for work or gaming. The keyboard even came with batteries, which was great! The wired option works well, too.

I didn't love the keycaps and replaced them right away with some that I already had, which are more smooth and have a broader key surface. That's a personal preference thing, though.

I've never used clear switches and was excited to give them a try. I usually use red switches, and found the clear switches to be very comfortable. They offer more resistance and shorter travel than red switches. Once I switched to lower profile key caps, I found the noise level to be slightly quieter as well. There is a bit of spring squeak when typing, though. There are also no feet on the keyboard to adjust the height/angle of your typing surface.

Only three of the switches can be removed, though I cannot figure out why you would... It removes functionality (why would you want a page up key, but not page down?). The only reason to do this that I could think of was to more distinctly isolate the arrow keys for gaming.

The keyboard came with a keycap puller and I was able to replace all of the caps without too much trouble. The keyboard is nice and compact and I do like that it's wireless. But for the price, it's very strange that there's no backlighting or truly hot-swappable switches.

reviews.title: Cherry Switches, dual USB/wireless, but missing other features
reviews.text:
I read the specifications several times for the Ganss keyboard, thinking I had missed something. Some things, in fact. The basic product -- the experience of typing on it -- is solid, and it's good to have both wireless and wired alternatives in the same keyboard.

But . . .

At this price point, you are going to do without several features that you might want. See CON.

But what you do get is a compact (no numeric pad, no function key row) keyboard whose touch I would judge to be halfway between gamer ("clacky") and laptop membrane-like (near-silent, minimal key feedback, short throw). The keys are durable, rollover-tolerant, likely top of the line at the key level.

PRO
- Compact
- Durable, with keytop replacement tool supplied
- Well-considered "middle feel" tactile design
- Status lights are below the keyboard, which makes them more visible when accidentally set (e.g., caps lock)
- Molded plastic cover will probably last awhile

CON
- Better keep the supplied manual (an annotated 2-pp diagram), as you may not be able to figure out key combinations after not using this for awhile. Function key access, for instance, is anything but intuitive
- Limited programmability
- No USB hub/auxiliary port (for mouse)
- Not backlit (requires different model from this maker)
- Tilt feature I usually use didn't work well for me YMMV
- I miss having an alternate ENTER key on the right edge somewhere
- The DEL key is oddly placed

Despite these limitations, you might like this model for the feel.

reviews.title: There are Much Better Options for the Price
reviews.text: This board was okay but at $100 it's hard to justify it's price. The build quality is similar to your run of the mill gk61 or sk61, so it's a plastic tray mount case with an some kind of metal plate not sure if it's steel or aluminum. The board isn't hotswap aside from 3 keys in the nav cluster on the right side of the board that can be covered with a plastic plate. I personally think Ganss was really brave to offer Cherry MX Clears as an option as those bottom out at nearly 100g but I'm here for it. The acoustics of the board are pretty bad this thing is loud and I would not recommend it for an office environment unless you really hate your coworkers. The stabilizers were sufficiently lubed and fit well in the plate so they didn't rattle. The keycaps seem like a cheap dye sub set and weren't terrible. The board lacks any kind of backlighting which I find strange as it has north facing switches which is usually the reason to have them that way otherwise they can interference with row 3 of cherry profile keycaps. The board has Bluetooth but doesn't have a rechargeable battery included, just 2 AAA Duracell batteries which I will give them credit for as Duracell is a good brand. Overall the board doesn't really do anything to justify it's steep price point there are much better boards in it's price range that include better switches, backlighting, and way better acoustics.

reviews.title: Compact, Well-Made
reviews.text:
This super-compact mechanical keyboard has a solid feel for a plastic keyboard at this price point. The travel and build quality of the keys feel great, and as expected for MX Clears (which are softer than other Cherry MX switches I have used). Having a detachable USB-C cable plus wireless is the best of all worlds for connectivity and long-term usability, and should be the standard for all keyboards moving forward.

I prefer a larger footprint for my daily driver, but this is a great keyboard if you need portability or work in a small space, and still want the benefits of Cherry MX switches.

n.b. The keyboard is not backlit, which increased battery life, but definitely reduces its "gamer chic" vibe.

title: ikbc W200 Wireless Mechanical Keyboard with Cherry MX Red Switch for Windows and Mac OS, Enables Media Key and LED Indicator (2.4G Dongle, USB 2.0, PBT Double Shot 87 Keycaps, Black Color, ANSI/US)
features: ▼ Mechanical keyboard with Cherry MX Red Switches (Linear): A linear, lighter but does not have tactile bump and audible click found comparing with Cherry MX Blue switches. Cherry MX Red is ideal for gaming where the low articulation force and linear path allow for quick successive key presses. Typing with Cherry MX Red switches is similar to browns, though it does lack tactile feedback and It is always the best preference for gamers.
▼ A durable computer keyboard with PBT Double Shot keycaps for Ergonomic Keycap Shapes designed to provide you with durable, long lasting and comfortable typing experience.

▼ Plug-And-Play Simplicity. : Setup takes seconds—you simply plug the wireless receiver into a USB port and start using your keyboard right away without software. 100% Anti-ghosting with 6-key Rollover and convenient FN Multimedia Shortcuts Keys.

▼ Other Features : Original Cherry MX Switch-Each Reliable Switches Rated for 50 million Typing Times. Using 2.4Ghz Wireless can Connectivity-Up to 6 Month Long Standby Time. Product structure include the Smart Power Indicator Light and 2 Adjustable Feetstand Height.
▼ What You Can Get: iKBC Cherry MX Red switch mechanical keyboard x1, 2.4G wireless dongle x1 (equipped inside battery area), AAA batteries x2, dust cover x1, key puller x1, user manual x1.

reviews.title: Great
reviews.text:
It meets all my needs. I got it to replace a wireless Velocifire keyboard whose internal battery was dying. I had to plug my old one in everyday as I couldn’t see the key labels without the backlight, and it’s built in battery life decreased from 5 to 1 days over a couple years. So it was essentially wired, while I prefer the wireless aesthetic.

This one avoids the backlight killing the battery life as well as internal battery degradation. I can see the key labels fine without a backlight, and it takes replaceable batteries. Battery life should be months without the backlight, and it even has a low battery warning light. No cord to deal with.

Seems well built, heavy, and doesn’t slide on the desk. Nice price point. Easy to set up. It even has multiple tilt angle (height) options, although I like it flat. TKL is great to minimize the distance to reach the mouse, improving ergonomics.

I went from brown to red switches as that’s all that was in stock, and it was an easy change, although I could tell a slight difference initially.

reviews.title: Quite Loud
reviews.text:
If you can't stand the sound of a clacking keyboard, this isn't for you. It can get quite loud, especially when you're typing fairly quickly. This is probably better for techies who like the intense sound and the tactile key interface. The lighting is also nice. I can recommend this keyboard for those who either live or work alone.

Thanks so much for taking the time to read this review. I hope the information that I've provided helps make your decision a little easier.

reviews.title: A No-Cable Classic For Writers
reviews.text:
I am not lying: This is hands down the best keyboard for writing I have used in the last twenty years. I liked the older acrylic Apple keyboard and I own a mechanical keyboard with click-switches (blue), but this one makes touch typing so much more comfortable.
I wasn’t sure if I would like the tactile keys without click, because I actually like the click. They are just very loud. Logitech’s K845 (with Cherry MX red) is a great keyboard as well, but louder and has a cable.
I wish this keyboard would be backlit though. Black keyboards in front of big screens need some sort of illumination. I got one of these USB LED lights that I plugged behind my monitor, that half-solves the problem by providing some backlight.
Still it’s awesome to write on it. It’s focus is to be a mechanical keyboard. So much that it seems that it skipped the product design. It looks like an OEM-Keyboard from 1995. That’s in a way uncompromisingly functional, but next to a modern flat butterfly keyboard it looks like someone parked an old Volvo next to a Miata.
The quality is good. The keycaps are heavy and lettering sharp. It might be confusing for some that the ‘upper’ characters are printed next to the main set in the same size.
Battery life seems not to be a problem. I am pretty much writing all day, and I’m still on the first set of batteries that came in the package.
PROS AND CONS
+ Precise keyboard for touch-typing
+ Quiet enough for shared work areas
+ Height adjustable
+ Great quality
+ Long Battery life
+ Connects via dongle, not Bluetooth (yes, for me that’s a pro. Bluetooth sucks)
+ Compact with no number pad

- Chunky design
- No backlight
- Very heavy
- Vague keycap lettering
- No number pad (*conflict*)
I can recommend this keyboard to anyone who is typing over a long amount of times. It takes a while to get used to, but once adjusted it’s less tiring than modern butterfly or rubber cap keyboards, because the keys don’t bottom out that easily, and feel better to the fingertips. I took a few photos. I hope it helps to make a decision.

reviews.title: Silent Cherry Red Switches Work Well With Family Room Computer
reviews.text:
While the cherry MX red keys are often used for gaming, I am reviewing this as a typing keyboard in a family room, where the loud clackity clack of a keyboard is unwelcome. And I find this keyboard to be a great solution for such an environment.

This keyboard paired so effortlessly with my Mac that I wasn’t even aware it happened. I checked the keys for their mapping, and found them to be as one would expect for a Windows keyboard—just the usual issue with the non-Apple standard keys near the Shift key.

It is not “full-size,” but 80% size, so it is somewhat smaller, but not so much so that you fee cramped.

They keys are as silent as advertised. And while an old-school touch typist might miss the “click” feedback of a mechanical keyboard, these keys have decent travel, so if you are typing, you will have little doubt that you have actually engaged the key. (I can’t talk to gaming with these.)

I have had no trouble with the bluetooth connection, nor the responsiveness while typing.

If I have any reliability issues, I will be sure to revisit this review as necessary.

🧡WARM TIMPS-Keycaps only, the keyboard in the picture is only for the demo and is not for sale. ANSI, US layout. This set cannot be used for 64/68/75/84/96 layout which need special keys such as “Shift”，“Alt” etc.

Usually on cheaper sets like this the legending and print quality can vary quite a bit but these keycaps were really consistent and is on par with some other sets I've had that cost twice as much so that was nice to see. Overall they're pretty great for the price and I honestly recommend them if you if you're looking for "designer" style keycaps.

I dont use this board often even when working in photoshop and really just use this board and keycap set as a decorative piece to add to my collection of keyboards. I love these keycaps!!

title: nuphy Air60 Portable Wireless Keyboard, 60% Low Profile Mechanical Keyboard, Supports Bluetooth 5.0, 2.4G and Wired Connection, Compatible with Windows and Mac OS Systems-Gateron Brown Switch
features: ✨【The new upgrade】：NuType F1 is highly praised by many users. On this basis, we have upgraded NuType F1 in all aspects and got the latest Air60 low-shaft mechanical keyboard. It adopts 60% layout (64 keys), bright color scheme, and the design of low shaft will not hurt your wrist after using the keyboard for a long time. The wide keycap design can help reduce the number of keystroke errors.

💻【Three-mode design】：Each user's usage is different. Based on user needs, we try to adapt the Air60 to as many users as possible, so the Air60 keyboard supports 2.4G, Bluetooth wireless connection and type C wired connection, and can connect up to 4 equipments at the same time to meet more complex user needs.

💪【Alternative laptop keyboard】：The four foot pads on the back of the Air60 can be perfectly embedded between the keys of the notebook keyboard. Typing on the Air60 will not touch the notebook keyboard at the bottom, giving notebook users a better typing experience. It is compatible with most laptops on the market, including the latest Macbooks.

💡【60% Portable Keyboard】：Compared with Air75, Air60 has no F area and will be smaller in size. If you don’t use F area often, Air60 is undoubtedly a better choice for use on the go; if you often use a laptop, Air60 can be perfectly placed. On the keyboard of the notebook computer, it is currently suitable for the vast majority of computers on the market, especially the Mac series, and it is more convenient to carry.

🔋【2500mAh battery】：The battery size of the Air60 wireless keyboard is 2500 mAh. The laboratory test time is more than 48 hours, which can meet the needs of a week of office work. When the backlight mode is turned off, the use time can be greatly increased.

reviews.title: Works great. Battery life is pathetic
reviews.text: Keyboard works great. Compact. Keys are responsible with good travel. The battery, however, lasts just about a day, which is unacceptable.

reviews.title: Didn't think low profile could feel this good...
reviews.text:
I was hesitant to try out this keyboard because I have always avoided low profile mechanical keyboards.

Maybe because I did actually type very well and fast with the chic-lit style keyboards that this keyboard reminds me of and I am too deep into the mechanical keyboard hobby to really want to move to low profile.

I am glad I took the leap!

I really like the way the browns feel on this keyboard. It is a pleasure to type on and the space bars and other stabilized keys feel great. Out of the box I don't think there is anything you need to do to this keyboard except use it!

Some things will take getting use to like the smaller shift button. I actually wouldn't mind if this board was spread out a bit more to allow for more traditional length buttons, but I'm sure it is something I could pick up quickly.

The build quality feels excellent and the polished steel plate on the bottom is a great touch. It really looks classy and I love the look of this keyboard. Nuphy's color-ways have always been super slick to me and this is no different.

The board has awesome RGB effects that you can change easily from the board itself. It connects wired, through bluetooth, or through a 2.4ghz adapter. Bluetooth connected instantly and works super well.

It comes with a ton of accessories, from magnetic feet to tilt the board a bit to extra keycaps, pullers, switches, stickers, and the instructions are also a poster of an anime chick which would probably look good in a gamer style room.

I am really tempted to try and use this board as a daily driver but right now I'm using it with it sitting on my M1 Macbook Airs keyboard and it is working great for that use case. It is thin enough that it doesn't block the screen or anything like that.

I have another compact 60% board that has the same goals as this Nuphy board and it misses the mark. Nuphy really hit the nail on the head with this board.

Ultimately it could have been a nice keyboard but it fell short.

reviews.title: Can be used stock!
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:528307786b36a4c2c9833ddd389101cb]] They come with a small amount of lube pre applied. Usually, prelubed switches are inconsistent in my experience but these ones are really good. I decided to not lube them at all. EDIT: got a few more batches, they're not as consistent. But seller was nice enough to get them replaced.

The stems are long pole. As a result, it has a nice bottom out sound.

It was noticeably heavier coming from a 50g switch to these that are 63g, but overtime I got adjusted and had no problems typing with them.

Overall a great buy, though I wish it was priced more competitively.

Using on Drop Preonic with MT3 Camillo, plateless, tape mod

reviews.title: Nice linears
reviews.text:
Starting off not sure who's at fault for this as it's only fulfilled by Amazon but one of my bags was missing a few switches (one bag was open and all the switches were rattling around inside the amazon pouch it arrived in) I counted 5 missing and there wasn't a silica gel packet either, the other bags were fine.
complaint aside they feel really nice and the factory lube seems to be well done as wish they had a "3 pin" option and different switch amounts 35 2 is too few and 3 is too many (unless some get lost in transit like mine lol) 90 would be a good option for the TKL/75% gang

reviews.title: Will be Using Right Away
reviews.text: In building & repairing computers these key switches are used very often. Mainly for high-end (over $100) keyboards that the serious gamers use. What they do is tough on the keyboards and often switches break or become less responsive which may lead to a gamer losing a match. These snap right in to the sockets and can instantly rebuild the whole keyboard like new. I realize they would not be used by your everyday person or casual gamer but when it gets serious they are  the switches to be used.

reviews.title: Nice Linear
reviews.text: These are nice and smooth and sound wonderful.  I'm not a linear fan usually, but I do like them in my modifier keys (shift, backspace, enter, and space) because I don't like the return sound a tactile makes while in a larger key.  These are perfect for that as they don't have a high pitch and there's no spring ping to even think of!  I would highly recommend them to anyone looking for a lighter-weight linear switch with some style.

reviews.title: Great Stock!
reviews.text: The switches sounds great and feels good to use. The lube in this is not bad at all and you don't hear any ping or scratchiness. The bottom out is much more than I am used to, but I do enjoy it otherwise.

reviews.title: Best keycaps I have ever used.
reviews.text:
Bought these for my GMMK pro.

I have owned several mechanical keyboards, but never bought a high-end set of keycaps. Unlike every other set I've ever used, these just seem to ignore dirt and grease. I can stuff my face with a big Mac and go straight to typing, and the caps stay completely clean. I don't know how that works but I won't question it.

Other keyboards require weekly wipe downs, I have never had to do it with these caps.

They're also noticeably built well. These are going to outlast my fingers by a hundred years, likely.

Everyone who posted reviews with pics show their legends glowing, so I’m not sure why mine are different. My other issue is I got several duplicative keys, and I’m missing a few. For example, I only have one home key and about 4 page down keys. So that’s slightly annoying. I’ve seen some others complain about this similar thing as well. But the feel and look of these key caps are quality. Slightly disappointed.

The keycap is PBT double shot OEM profile.The thickness is around 1.2mm
It can be used for most standard ANSI Cherry MX Gateron MX Kailh MX mechanical keyboard whose bottom is 1x6.25u space + 7x1.25u
It can be used for KBD75 YMD75 Tada68 XD64 GH60 Keychron K2 K6
It can be compatible with YMD96 Melody 96 Layout C

reviews.title: Shine through is very disappointing
reviews.text: I got these primarily because I liked the orange and black color scheme and the advertised shine through features but the backlit brightness is only fair to poor on these (I'm being generous giving it 3 stars, I think).  Even the cheap $6.00 keycap set ($0.38 after discounts) with a similar color scheme I got from aliexpress is brighter than these.  However, since I need those 1u-sized right Alt, FN and Ctrl keycaps for my Royal Kludge RK96 90% keyboard, I'm not returning therm, though I basically paid $20 for those 3 keycaps :/

title: Sumgsn 172 Keys Double Shot Apollo Keycaps Cherry Profile for 61/64/87/104/108 Cherry Mx Switches ISO/ANSI Layout Mechanical Keyboard
features: Production Methods-These keycaps are made of double shot craft, with good texture and clear legends, with a thickness of approximately 1.5mm, and will not be backlit.c
Wide Compatibility-It has a total of 172 keys, including various sizes of keycaps suitable for most cherry mx switches 60% 65% 95% ISO/ANSI layout mechanical keyboards.
Profile-These keycaps are ergonomically designed Cherry profile keycaps that can provide you with a comfortable typing experience, better for work and gaming.
Packaing-This product is packaged with ABS keycaps tray with tray cover and comes with a key extractor, which can effectively protect the keycaps during transportation and avoid scratches and stains.
Warm Tips-Only keycaps, not a keyboard. if the keycaps missing or broken, please contact customer service to send you new keys.

reviews.title: Great set
reviews.text: Bought these for a budget build, my set was perfect. Quality exceeded expectations.

reviews.title: Spectacular!
reviews.text:
all i have to say is, get these keycaps. i was truly surprised at the quality of these keycaps. they feel really nice, solid, and with a little grain on the top that are very comfortable. Pricewise i think these are right on. At about $50 the quality is really good without the wait and the much higher prices of some other premium ABS...the colorway is one that i really like. I have always had a handful of colorways i wanted, the botonical, these Hammerhead, modern dolch, etc. the legends are pretty consistent with a few fonts thinner than others but i count 2 keys in my 60% board. not bad and i don't really notice it. i really like the color scheme more than i would notice the font issues.

Now i only wish i would have have a bigger board, 75% or TKL to take advantage of the other keys. geez keyboard world you will bankrupt me.

Very beautiful set. i'm coming back to my 60% for a little while.

reviews.title: Lovely colors, plenty of options!
reviews.text: Key caps look really great. Tons of combinations of caps for just about every layout. I threw it on an old WYSE WY-60 keyboard and it had some uncommon unit sizes for a few keys. Worked out great after finding keycaps that fit its spot. I had to grind down the edges of a few keys to fit them nicely. A modern keyboard will have no compatibility issues without a doubt!

reviews.title: Perfect
reviews.text: Although it was pricey I loved the color scheme and wanted it. It has extra bits like longer or shorter space bar and ctrl, alt keys in case your board needs them.

reviews.title: Quality keycaps
reviews.text:
Well built and produced keycaps. This keyboard includes keys for Aifei, not sure if they are the ones who made these keycaps or the ones they are cloning.

Very nice and bright colors, font looks great.

Though this set seems to have a bit of lower profile and standard keycaps. So just a heads up on that.

Overall I am happy with these Keycaps.

title: SteelSeries PRISMCAPS - Double Shot Pudding-Style Keycaps - Durable PBT Thermoplastic - Compatible with a Wide Range of Mechanical Keyboards - White
description: Built for extreme durability with thermoplastic PBT keycaps and with enhanced RGB pudding-style lighting, PrismCaps include 2 additional sets of bottom row keycaps, making them compatible with the widest range of MX stem mechanical keyboards.
features: Ultra-wide compatibility with both standard and non-standard bottom row keycap sets to fit most mechanical keyboard brands, including SteelSeries, Razer, Corsair, and virtually all other MX stem mechanical keyboards
Standard US ANSI 104 keys works on full sized, tenkeyless, and 60% mini keyboard layouts
Enhanced lighting displays brilliant pudding-style RGB illumination through a specially engineered double shot design with translucent sides and letters
Retains consistent texture and appearance even after heavy use with highly durable double shot PBT keycaps
Included keycap puller is built with finger hole grip to pull any keycap out with ease and flexible enough to fit around any standard keycap.

reviews.title: for apex pro
reviews.text: bought these for my apex pro. they fit perfect and i definitely like them better than the caps that came with the keyboard

reviews.title: Great keys for $25
reviews.text: I really like these keys because it is very nicely built. The plastic is thick and there is some texture. The only problem with the texture part is that the A,W,S,D buttons are going to wear down and it will smooth out. I would appreciate if they can do something to make it more durable. I’d still recommend this for putty caps.

reviews.title: Great keycaps
reviews.text: i got these for my Apex Pro they look amazing on it about the only thing about them is more about the apex then the keycaps,the space bar only has one led on the keyboard in the center so does not light up the spacebar as much as the rest of the keys and is pretty noticeable...the kit came with everything needed to be used on several different keyboards that i have so i might get another set for my corsair boards next.

reviews.title: They look so good!
reviews.text: Made my Steelseries Apex 7 keyboard pop even more. They feel great with my Red Linear Switches. They are a little more expensive than some other brands, but I feel like it was better to go with the same brand since I have seen other people have issues with switching out the keycaps on Steelseries keyboards.

reviews.title: Love these!
reviews.text: Amazing PBT pudding keycaps, I absolutely love these! I chose these to match my SteelSeries keyboard and I'm very happy with them.

title: Wireless Gaming Keyboard and Mouse Combo, 104 Keys Standard US Layout RGB Backlit Rechargeable Mechanical Feel Waterproof Mute Mouse for Gamers(White)
features: 【2 in 1 wireless keyboard and mouse set】：This combo includes a 104-key Standard American layout gaming keyboard and a light-up gaming mouse. The keyboard and mouse both support charging, and a common charging cable is included in the package.

【2.4Ghz wireless connection】：The keyboard and mouse share a dongle, the dongle is located at the bottom of the mouse, the maximum support for 32 feet using distance. (Please open the keyboard and mouse switch before use)
【RGB backlit keyboard】 : Keyboard is equipped with FN+(1-5) 5 groups of shortcut keys to adjust the keyboard lighting in 19 color modes. Keyboard brightness adjustable, night use is not affected.

【Wireless Gaming Mouse】: This wireless mouse features a cool crackle pattern and 7-color auto-change breathing backlight design, 7 buttons, symmetrical design of the mouse, and 20 million keystrokes life.

【Plug and Play】:The keyboard and mouse are factory charged and paired, just turn on the keyboard and mouse switch and insert the dongle to use. (Note: If you lose the connection, please complete the charge, reset the pairing code pairing can be)

reviews.title: Pretty, but this WILL break within 3 months
reviews.text: Very low quality. I've replaced everything once before and now need another but no warranty.

reviews.title: Needs a dimmer or more contrast
reviews.text: Hard to see the key characters when lit

reviews.title: Nice Product
reviews.text: I recommend the black keyboard, for one reason: it lights up the keys, showing the letters, and the numbers. With the white keyboard, and the light it just doesn't show the letters and numbers as easily because the light against the white is too much alike. I returned this for this reason only: great buying/returning experience. I recommend this seller. The product is very nice, and of good quality.

reviews.title: Broken Already...
reviews.text: I bought this back in October 2022. Off and on switch broke off. Keyboard is stuck in the on position.

reviews.title: Lasted two months. Performed great for two months suddenly died.
reviews.text: Loved until it died an early death.

title: Mosptnspg CJXSP PBT 61 Keycaps 60 Percent, Gaming Keycaps OEM Profile Backlit Keycap Set for 60 Percent Cherry MX Mechanical Keyboard GK61 ，Only keycaps (skyce)
description: This 61 mechanical Keyboard caps has a perfect size and it's good for Home and Office use, more practical for Gamers and Typist, give more space to your desktop. Specification: 1. Comfortable Design : With high-quality PBT material and an anti-grease coating, these keycaps provide top-notch durability and a great feel for your typing
High quality PBT keycaps : The thickened keycap wall makes the keycap more sturdy and durable. The quality is not lost to those expensive keycaps. OEM profile, higher than Cherry profile

reviews.title: So far so good! Note to buyers with costar stabilizers
reviews.text: Nice quality for the price, easy to install, keys came in a plastic tray so you can store your old keycaps in order if needed. Note to those with costar stabilizers, the key caps do not come with MX Keycap Stabilizer Inserts... so if your old keys are like mine and the old ones are apparently glued in, you'll need these to finish the keyboard. You can find them to 3d print pretty easily.

reviews.title: Printed partially
reviews.text: The letters are not oriented fully. Bothers me because I can lines through the letters.

reviews.title: I type so well on these
reviews.text: I didn't think a different key profile would do much for me, but my accuracy immediately went up with the XDA profile. These were a great price among the similar sets I found and were an affordable way to dive into my first customization. I wanted some kinda retro keys and I just love em!
